source: .claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scenario-meanings.md → token-accounting (multi-turn token accumulation in one interactive codex session)
notes: Three back-to-back no-op turns ('Reply with exactly: ok') in one live codex REPL session, stressing per-turn token accumulation. Unlike headless `codex exec` (single-shot, ends in process_exited), the interactive driver keeps the rollout session alive across all three turns, so the daemon observes three full ready→working→ready arcs on the SAME rollout session_id, then process_exited only when the driver tears the tmux REPL down. The daemon sees proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then per-turn working/ready flips. Each turn's _done anchors to its own _start (not to session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not an earlier handoff. The token-accounting evidence the scenario validates lives in events.jsonl payloads, not the phase DSL (which has no metric form): codex emits a token_count event_msg per turn carrying total_token_usage; the parser's cumulative cursor → per-turn delta math emits a PerTurnContribution (input/output/cache UsageBreakdown) on each boundary and SessionMetrics.TotalTokens accumulates monotonically (TotalTokens>0 after T1; ≥ prior total after T2 and T3). cached_tokens (input_tokens_details.cached_tokens) is routed to the cache bucket and deducted from Input to avoid double-count. Cost may read $0 if the LiteLLM pricing cache lacks the model — a pricing-cache limitation, not a token-accounting gap; the recipe asserts token counts/deltas, which are real and observable. Covered by parser unit test TestParser_MultiTurnTokenCount_UsesPerTurnSnapshot.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
First user prompt → working; turn 1 produces the first per-turn token contribution (TotalTokens > 0 after T1)
First turn ends → back to ready (state reset between turns)
Second user prompt → working on the same session; turn 2 adds another contribution (TotalTokens after T2 ≥ TotalTokens after T1, monotonic)
Second turn ends → back to ready
Third user prompt → working on the same session; turn 3 adds a contribution (TotalTokens after T3 ≥ TotalTokens after T2, monotonic across 3 turns)
Third turn ends in ready; session never enters waiting across the conversation
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
